headline: American Express releases monthly delinquency and write-off statistics for U.S. Consumer and Small Business Card Member loans for February 2026.
investor_impact: U.S. Consumer 30-day delinquencies remained flat month-over-month at 1.4%, while net write-offs ticked up slightly to 2.0%, signaling stable but normalizing credit conditions. These metrics are critical for analysts modeling near-term provision expenses and assessing overall consumer health.
key_figures: Total U.S. Consumer Card Member loans (Feb 28, 2026): $95.1 billion
U.S. Consumer 30 days past due rate (Feb 28, 2026): 1.4%
Total U.S. Small Business Card Member loans (Feb 28, 2026): $31.3 billion
U.S. Small Business 30 days past due rate (Feb 28, 2026): 1.7%
U.S. Small Business net write-off rate (Feb 28, 2026): 2.8%
Total Card Member loans held for investment (Feb 28, 2026): $126.4 billion
